title: Executive Order 12874—Establishing an Emergency Board To Investigate a Dispute Between The Long Island Rail Road and Certain of Its Employees Represented by the United Transportation Union

flags.justification: A three-member board with powers entirely defined by statute (Railway Labor Act §9A), with an explicit conflict-of-interest exclusion for anyone pecuniarily interested in railroad labor organizations or carriers.
Sec. 2 requires a report within thirty days of the board's creation.
Single purpose: investigating this one labor dispute.
The Board's powers and duties are entirely defined by the Railway Labor Act itself.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.

Sec. 4 explicitly provides automatic, self-executing termination upon submission of the required report — cleaner than EO 10842's comparable Taft-Hartley board, which required affirmative future presidential action to terminate.
This exercises the statutory Railway Labor Act emergency-board process exactly as Congress designed it.
The Railway Labor Act's broader framework, including judicial review provisions, provides real external checks.
A routine, statutory dispute-resolution mechanism; no novel authority claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/18 = 0.0%. Notably cleaner in its termination design than its Taft-Hartley counterpart, EO 10842.
